2604.18786.title: Experiments or Outcomes? Probing Scientific Feasibility in Large Language Models
2604.18786.paper_summary: Scientific feasibility assessment asks whether a claim is consistent with established knowledge and whether experimental evidence could support or refute it. We frame feasibility assessment as a diagnostic reasoning task in which, given a hypothesis, a model predicts feasible or infeasible and justifies its decision. We evaluate large language models (LLMs) under controlled knowledge conditions (hypothesis-only, with experiments, with outcomes, or both) and probe robustness by progressively removing portions of the experimental and/or outcome context. Across multiple LLMs and two datasets, providing outcome evidence is generally more reliable than providing experiment descriptions. Outcomes tend to improve accuracy beyond what internal knowledge alone provides, whereas experimental text can be brittle and may degrade performance when the context is incomplete. These findings clarify when experimental evidence benefits LLM-based feasibility assessment and when it introduces fragility.
2604.18786.paper_summary_zh: 科學可行性評估詢問一個主張是否與既有知識一致，以及實驗證據是否能支持或反駁該主張。<lb/>我們將可行性評估框架設為一種診斷推理任務，在這個任務中，給定一個假設，模型預測可行或不可行並為其決策提供理由。<lb/>我們在受控知識條件下（僅假設、有實驗、有結果或兩者皆有）評估大型語言模型（LLMs），並通過逐步去除實驗和/或結果上下文的部分來探測其穩健性。<lb/>在多個LLMs和兩個數據集上，提供結果證據通常比提供實驗描述更可靠。<lb/>結果往往能提高準確性，超過僅依賴內部知識所能提供的準確性，而實驗文本可能脆弱，當上下文不完整時可能會降低性能。<lb/>這些發現澄清了實驗證據何時有助於基於LLM的可行性評估，以及何時會引入脆弱性。
2604.18786.author: Seyedali Mohammadi et.al.
2604.18786.authors: Seyedali Mohammadi, Manas Gaur, Francis Ferraro
